L

T Tauri

System / Location · First seen 3307-05-07 · 13 mentions
EDSM ↗ Inara ↗
Active: 3307-05-07 → 3309-01-30
System Overview

T Tauri is a system that became a battleground in the internal conflict over Salvation's anti-Thargoid campaign in late 3307. In November 3307, Admiral Aden Tanner led a rogue Aegis Defense force against Taurus Mining Ventures in the system, attempting to seize Hind Mine asteroid base to obtain evidence that Salvation's superweapon was deliberately designed to provoke the Thargoids. Taurus Mining Ventures defeated Tanner's forces, captured the Musashi megaship, and placed Tanner and key personnel in temporary custody.

The battle in T Tauri represented a critical fracture in human anti-xeno strategy — pitting Aegis's official military leadership against Salvation's corporate allies. Tanner's defeat and capture marked the nadir of Aegis's operational independence, while Taurus Mining Ventures' victory cemented its role as Salvation's primary military proxy. The system thus stands as a symbol of how corporate-paramilitary forces eclipsed traditional superpower militaries during the Thargoid crisis.

System Data
Coordinates: [-32.96875, -206.40625, -557.3125]
Allegiance: Independent
Government: Corporate
Controlling Faction: Azimuth Biotech
Security: Medium
Economy: Extraction
Population: 21.3K

Mentions Timeline (13 articles)
3309-01-30
Admiral Tanner Withdraws from Federal Navy
Admiral Aden Tanner resigns from the Federal Navy weeks after his presidential pardon, citing bureaucratic restrictions that prevent him from effectively combating the Thargoid threat.
3308-11-15
Coalsack Nebula Reports Thargoid Activity
The human colonists in the Coalsack Nebula has raised the alarm after Thargoid forces appeared several systems.
3308-09-16
The Judgement of Admiral Tanner
The Federal Navy Criminal Court upholds Admiral Aden Tanner's conviction for mutiny but commutes his sentence to time served, acknowledging that preventing the Proteus Wave might have saved many lives.
3308-09-01
Supply Mined Materials to Azimuth Biotech
Azimuth Biotech launches a mining campaign to deliver raw materials to the Glorious Prospect megaship in LHS 157, seeking to rebuild corporate infrastructure after being forced out of Maia, Merope and Qarato.
3308-08-30
A New Chapter for Azimuth
Azimuth Biotech announces a new chapter under acting CEO Torben Rademaker after Salvation's death, with the Musashi returning to T Tauri and the Glorious Prospect jumping to LHS 157 to establish a resource-gathering initiative.
3308-08-26
Retrial Requested for Admiral Tanner
The Federal Attorney's Office calls for Admiral Aden Tanner's conviction to be reviewed in light of HIP 22460, where events validated his accusation that Salvation deliberately attracted Thargoids to test his superweapon.
3308-08-25
Report from HIP 22460: Salvation is Dead
An independent rescue team confirms that Salvation, identified as Dr Caleb Wycherley, was killed aboard the Bright Sentinel in HIP 22460, found dead in his command chair surrounded by shattered cybernetic implants.
3308-07-25
Azimuth Condemns 'Alien-Loving' Resistance
Azimuth Biotech director Torben Rademaker condemns Operation Wych Hunt protestors as alien-loving traitors after Azimuth assets are expelled from Merope and Qarato and Hind Mine is attacked.
3308-06-28
Azimuth: A Historical Perspective (Part Four)
Part four traces the escalating rivalry between Aegis and Salvation, Admiral Tanner's failed assault on Hind Mine and court martial, the simultaneous invasions of Delphi, Maia and Merope, and the discovery of Alexandria debris revealing Azimuth's survival.
3308-06-28
Azimuth A Historical Perspective (Part Three)
Part three covers Salvation's emergence in April 3307, his recruitment of pilots through Taurus Mining Ventures, the Cornsar incident where his prototype superweapon repelled a Thargoid fleet, and his rise as a rival to Aegis.
3308-06-24
Azimuth: A Historical Perspective (Part Two)
Part two traces Aegis from its 3303 creation through the Alexandria disaster, Admiral Tanner's rogue assault on Hind Mine, and its dissolution following the Baumann Report.
3307-06-11
The Legacy of the Hesperus
Pilots report new contact from 'Salvation' near the Hesperus and Adamastor. Commander Tyler Thorsen describes receiving a request to locate old signal beacons that could help find survivors from the megaship. The Pilots Federation cautions against working for potentially criminal organisations.
3307-05-07
The Voice of 'Salvation'
The mysterious figure 'Salvation' sends a message to pilots who discovered the Hesperus, authorising Taurus Mining Ventures to pay for logs and promising further work. He claims his scientific breakthroughs will 'literally save all of humanity'.
